benchmark_run.summary: 2 benchmarks executed successfully in Dry mode

requirements.title: Allow key update after handshake confirmation
requirements.remaining_gap: The repo still lacks handshake-confirmation state and the 1-RTT key-update subsystem needed to initiate a key update safely.
requirements.notes: Leave this blocked until the key-update state machine exists.

requirements.title: Identify packet protection keys with Key Phase
requirements.notes: The existing short-header parser already surfaces the Key Phase bit; the updated RFC 9001 tags now make that evidence traceable.

requirements.title: Initialize Key Phase to zero
requirements.remaining_gap: The repo does not yet format 1-RTT packets with a key-update state machine, so the initial Key Phase value is not a live implementation concern yet.
requirements.notes: Leave this blocked until packet protection and 1-RTT packet formatting exist.

requirements.title: Toggle Key Phase on each update
requirements.remaining_gap: The repo does not yet have a key-update state machine that can toggle Key Phase across successive updates.
requirements.notes: This depends on stateful 1-RTT key management.

requirements.title: Let Key Phase detect key changes
requirements.remaining_gap: The repo does not yet have a receiver-side key-update state machine that can detect a change in keying material from Key Phase alone.
requirements.notes: This requires receiver-side 1-RTT packet protection state.

requirements.title: Update keys when Key Phase changes
requirements.remaining_gap: The repo does not yet have key-update logic that can swap packet-protection keys when the Key Phase bit changes.
requirements.notes: Leave this blocked until the packet-protection subsystem exists.

requirements.title: Decrypt the packet with the changed Key Phase
requirements.remaining_gap: The repo does not yet implement packet decryption or the changed-Key-Phase recovery path needed by this clause.
requirements.notes: This depends on a real packet-protection and recovery implementation.

requirements.title: Update both endpoints on key update
requirements.remaining_gap: The repo does not yet model bilateral key-update propagation between endpoints.
requirements.notes: A connection-state machine is required before this can be implemented cleanly.

requirements.title: Prohibit TLS KeyUpdate messages
requirements.remaining_gap: The repo does not yet have a TLS message-processing surface where outbound KeyUpdate messages could be prohibited.
requirements.notes: Leave this blocked until TLS integration exists.

requirements.title: Treat TLS KeyUpdate as a connection error
requirements.remaining_gap: The repo does not yet have TLS alert handling or connection-error orchestration for received KeyUpdate messages.
requirements.notes: This depends on the missing TLS handshake and connection error pipeline.

requirements.title: Use caution with unauthenticated Initial data
requirements.remaining_gap: This is policy guidance about unauthenticated Initial data, not a clean executable invariant in the current helper-only surfaces.
requirements.notes: Keep it deferred until there is a richer handshake or policy surface to attach it to.

requirements.title: Fail on Handshake tampering
requirements.remaining_gap: The repo still lacks a TLS handshake implementation, so handshake tampering cannot be exercised or detected here.
requirements.notes: Leave this blocked until handshake transport exists.

requirements.title: Carry QUIC transport parameters
requirements.notes: The codec layer already carries transport-parameter values; the updated RFC 9001 tags make that helper-layer proof explicit.

requirements.title: Authenticate QUIC transport parameters
requirements.remaining_gap: The repo still lacks TLS transcript and authentication plumbing, so transport-parameter authentication cannot be proven cryptographically yet.
requirements.notes: The codec can carry bytes, but it does not authenticate them.

requirements.title: Apply TLS security considerations to QUIC
requirements.remaining_gap: This is a document-level security-scope rule rather than an executable helper behavior in the current repo.
requirements.notes: Keep it in the gap ledger until the repo has a broader TLS/security analysis surface.

requirements.title: Register quic_transport_parameters at codepoint 57
requirements.notes: The helper now exposes the registry codepoint as a public constant for traceability.

requirements.title: Mark Recommended as Yes
requirements.notes: The registry recommendation flag is now surfaced as a helper constant for traceability.

requirements.title: Include CH and EE in TLS 1.3 column
requirements.notes: The TLS 1.3 context metadata is surfaced as explicit helper constants rather than being left implicit.
